org.apache.flink.runtime.highavailability

## Interface HighAvailabilityServices

  * All Superinterfaces:
    [AutoCloseable](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/AutoCloseable.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang"), [ClientHighAvailabilityServices](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/highavailability/ClientHighAvailabilityServices.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.highavailability")

All Known Implementing Classes:
    [AbstractHaServices](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/highavailability/AbstractHaServices.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.highavailability"), [AbstractNonHaServices](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/highavailability/nonha/AbstractNonHaServices.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.highavailability.nonha"), [EmbeddedHaServices](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/highavailability/nonha/embedded/EmbeddedHaServices.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.highavailability.nonha.embedded"), [EmbeddedHaServicesWithLeadershipControl](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/highavailability/nonha/embedded/EmbeddedHaServicesWithLeadershipControl.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.highavailability.nonha.embedded"), [KubernetesHaServices](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/kubernetes/highavailability/KubernetesHaServices.html> "class in org.apache.flink.kubernetes.highavailability"), [StandaloneHaServices](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/highavailability/nonha/standalone/StandaloneHaServices.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.highavailability.nonha.standalone"), [ZooKeeperHaServices](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/highavailability/zookeeper/ZooKeeperHaServices.html> "class in org.apache.flink.runtime.highavailability.zookeeper")

* * *




        public interface HighAvailabilityServices
        extends [ClientHighAvailabilityServices](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/highavailability/ClientHighAvailabilityServices.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.highavailability")

The HighAvailabilityServices give access to all services needed for a highly-available setup. In particular, the services provide access to highly available storage and registries, as well as distributed counters and leader election.
    * ResourceManager leader election and leader retrieval
    * JobManager leader election and leader retrieval
    * Persistence for checkpoint metadata
    * Registering the latest completed checkpoint(s)
    * Persistence for the BLOB store
    * Registry that marks a job's status
    * Naming of RPC endpoints

  *     * ### Field Detail

      * #### DEFAULT_LEADER_ID

            static final [UUID](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/util/UUID.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.util") DEFAULT_LEADER_ID

This UUID should be used when no proper leader election happens, but a simple pre-configured leader is used. That is for example the case in non-highly-available standalone setups.

      * #### DEFAULT_JOB_ID

            static final [JobID](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/JobID.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common") DEFAULT_JOB_ID

This JobID should be used to identify the old JobManager when using the [`HighAvailabilityServices`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/highavailability/HighAvailabilityServices.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.highavailability"). With the new mode every JobMaster will have a distinct JobID assigned.

      * #### getWebMonitorLeaderRetriever

            [@Deprecated](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Deprecated.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")
            default [LeaderRetrievalService](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/leaderretrieval/LeaderRetrievalService.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.leaderretrieval") getWebMonitorLeaderRetriever()

Deprecated. just use [`getClusterRestEndpointLeaderRetriever()`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/highavailability/HighAvailabilityServices.html#getClusterRestEndpointLeaderRetriever-->) instead of this method.

This retriever should no longer be used on the cluster side. The web monitor retriever is only required on the client-side and we have a dedicated high-availability services for the client, named [`ClientHighAvailabilityServices`](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/highavailability/ClientHighAvailabilityServices.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.highavailability"). See also FLINK-13750.

Returns:
    the leader retriever for web monitor

      * #### close

            void close()
                throws [Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")

Closes the high availability services, releasing all resources.

This method **does not delete or clean up** any data stored in external stores (file systems, ZooKeeper, etc). Another instance of the high availability services will be able to recover the job.

If an exception occurs during closing services, this method will attempt to continue closing other services and report exceptions only after all services have been attempted to be closed.

Specified by:
    `[close](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/AutoCloseable.html?is-external=true#close--> "class or interface in java.lang")` in interface `[AutoCloseable](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/AutoCloseable.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")`
Throws:
    `[Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")` \- Thrown, if an exception occurred while closing these services.
      * #### closeAndCleanupAllData

            void closeAndCleanupAllData()
                                 throws [Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")

Closes the high availability services (releasing all resources) and deletes all data stored by these services in external stores.

After this method was called, the any job or session that was managed by these high availability services will be unrecoverable.

If an exception occurs during cleanup, this method will attempt to continue the cleanup and report exceptions only after all cleanup steps have been attempted.

Throws:
    `[Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")` \- Thrown, if an exception occurred while closing these services or cleaning up data stored by them.
      * #### cleanupJobData

            default void cleanupJobData([JobID](<../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/JobID.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common") jobID)
                                 throws [Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")

Deletes all data for specified job stored by these services in external stores.

Parameters:
    `jobID` \- The identifier of the job to cleanup.
Throws:
    `[Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")` \- Thrown, if an exception occurred while cleaning data stored by them.
